---
title: "Converting .tex or .docx Files to Markdown"
subtitle: ""
author: ""
date: ""
image: "https://pandoc.org/diagram.svgz" # <-- image to be used in links to slides
image-alt: Pandoc diogram # <- alt text for accessibility
format:
 revealjs: # see https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs/
   title-slide-attributes:
    data-background-image: "https://pandoc.org/diagram.svgz" # <-- background of title slide
    data-background-opacity: "0.5" # <-- background opacity of title slide
   theme: [../michigan.scss]
   scrollable: false
footer: "👈 Click on Block M for menu - [Return to the website](index.qmd)"
---

## Markdown is a *lightweight* syntax

- In contrast to LaTeX, you type [a lot less funny business](https://www.guillaumedehaene.com/posts/2024/03/quarto_is_better.html#quarto-improvements)
- In contrast to Microsoft Office, everything is plain text and in your control---no buttons or formatting issues on different machines

![](https://i.imgflip.com/253k2f.jpg "Join me in the light side meme")

## Markdown math is $\approxeq$ LaTeX

(but more accessible)

`Inline: $\sum_{i=0}^n i^2 = \frac{(n^2+n)(2n+1)}{6}$`

Inline: $\sum_{i=0}^n i^2 = \frac{(n^2+n)(2n+1)}{6}$

Display mode allows labeling:

`$$\sum_{i=0}^n i^2 = \frac{(n^2+n)(2n+1)}{6} \tag{1a}$$`

$$\sum_{i=0}^n i^2 = \frac{(n^2+n)(2n+1)}{6} \tag{1a}$$


<!--
## Pandoc converts between file types

![](https://pandoc.org/pandoc-cartoon.svgz "pandoc cartoon")
--> 

## Docx syllabus to markdown

1.  In terminal: `pandoc -t markdown -o syllabus.md syllabus.docx`
1.  Change ".md" to ".qmd" 
1.  Add a YAML header at the top:

```yaml
title: Syllabus
format:
 html: default
 pdf: default # delete this line if you don't want a PDF version rendered via LaTeX
```

(Just copy the header from [syllabus.qmd](https://github.com/judgelord/PP000/blob/main/syllabus.qmd))


## LaTeX syllabus to markdown

1.  In terminal: `pandoc -t markdown -o syllabus.md syllabus.tex`
1.  Change ".md" to ".qmd" 
1.  Add a YAML header at the top:

```yaml
title: Syllabus
format:
 html: default
 pdf: default # delete this line if you don't want a PDF version rendered via LaTeX
# docx: default # Do you really want a docx version, too? 
```

(Just copy the header from [syllabus.qmd](https://github.com/judgelord/PP000/blob/main/syllabus.qmd))

## Beamer to markdown

1.  In terminal: `pandoc -t markdown -o slides.md slides.tex`
1.  Change ".md" to ".qmd" 
1.  Add a YAML header at the top:

```yaml
  title: "My Slides"
  format:
    revealjs: 
      slide-number: true
```

(Just copy from the file that made these slides, [pandoc.qmd](https://github.com/judgelord/PP000/blob/main/slides/pandoc.qmd))

::: callout-tip
Pandoc can also do the reverse, of course, but .qmd can also render beamer

```yaml
format:
  beamer:
    keep-tex: true
    fontsize: 11pt         # optional
    toc: false             # optional
    slide-level: 2         # optional
```
:::
